summary: Sensor provider registry, ranked telemetry fallback, and UI refinement with no tuning/write expansion.

cpu_metric_behavior.temperature: 0 C is invalid_value and unavailable for headline/card use
cpu_metric_behavior.power: 0 W with nonzero CPU load is stale_zero/unavailable
cpu_metric_behavior.clock: 0 MHz with nonzero CPU load is stale_zero/unavailable; Windows counters may supply a lower-ranked valid fallback
cpu_metric_behavior.voltage: valid when in reasonable CPU voltage range

provider_registry.hwinfosm_probe: detects process and shared-memory mapping; full parser remains guarded
provider_registry.windows_counter_fallback: CPU load and CPU frequency/performance counters

ui_changes: CPU hero can use CPU load as primary when temperature is unavailable
CPU temperature fallback guidance block added with HWiNFO next action
raw explorer includes validity, validity reason, provider, and subcategory
CPU diagnostics export includes provider statuses, provider sensors, candidate lists, selected metrics, fallback chains, and unavailable reasons
Dashboard and Settings show Phase 8 provider status

latest_live_snapshot.cpu_temp_unavailable_reason: Core (Tctl/Tdie) reported 0 C and was rejected as invalid.
latest_live_snapshot.next_recommended_action: Start HWiNFO64 Sensors with shared memory enabled.
cpu_temperature_guidance: LHM rejected Core (Tctl/Tdie) because it reported 0 C.
HWiNFO was not running or shared memory was unavailable.
Windows counters are available for CPU load/frequency but do not provide CPU die temperature.
